annotations.label: Kepler's first law (lead)

anchor.snippet: The orbit of a planet is an ellipse with the Sun at one of the two foci

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Kepler-orbit or ellipse-shaped trajectory formalization (no hits for Kepler/ellipse).

annotations.label: Kepler's second law (lead)

anchor.snippet: A line segment joining a planet and the Sun sweeps out equal areas during equal intervals of time.

annotations.note: No equal-areas / areal-velocity law: no angular-momentum or central-force machinery in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kepler's third law (lead)

anchor.snippet: The square of a planet's orbital period is proportional to the cube of the length of the semi-major axis of its orbit.

annotations.note: T²∝a³ is not in Mathlib (no Kepler hits at all).

annotations.label: Eccentricity of Earth's orbit from equinox times

anchor.snippet: The eccentricity of the orbit of the Earth makes the time from the March equinox to the September equinox

annotations.note: No astronomical / orbital-mechanics computations in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kepler's third law

annotations.note: T²/a³ law absent from Mathlib.
